title: Medical walker
abstract: A medical walker apparatus and a method for using the medical walker. Embodiments of the medical walker include a wheeled frame and a compromised limb gait system attached to the wheeled frame, where the compromised limb gait system is configured to guide a limb of a user through a simulated gait motion. In aspects, the medical walker gives an amputee patient mobility by providing a leg brace support for the compromised limb with a flexible connection to the walker that guides the compromised limb through a biomechanically proper walking motion, thus allowing the compromised limb to be exercised.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A medical walker for use by a user with a compromised limb, comprising: a wheeled frame adapted to move along a floor surface and support at least a portion of the userâ s weight; and a compromised limb gait system attached to the wheeled frame, wherein the compromised limb gait system is configured to connect to the compromised limb of the user and guide the compromised limb through a simulated gait motion. 2. The medical walker of claim 1, wherein the compromised limb gait system comprises: a yoke that connects a brace to a compromised limb gait system, where the brace is connected to the compromised limb of the user; at least one swing linkage, wherein the swing linkage permits movement in substantially a single plane and connects the yoke to the wheeled frame; and a hard stop, wherein the hard stop limits the rearward motion of the swing linkage. 3. The medical walker of claim 2, wherein the brace is configured to conform to the limb of the user and wrap around a circumference of the compromised limb to avoid putting pressure on a surgical site on the compromised limb. 4. The medical walker of claim 3, wherein the brace further comprises a pseudo-foot protruding from the bottom of the brace that mimics the motion of a foot of a prosthetic limb. 5. The medical walker of claim 1, wherein the compromised limb gait system comprises: a plurality of parallel arms, wherein each of the parallel arms connects to the wheeled frame at one end of the arms and connects to a brace at the opposite end of the arms, wherein the brace is attached to the compromised limb of the user; and a plurality of channels fixed to the brace that allow smooth movement of the brace through a normal walking gait when connected to the parallel arms. 6. The medical walker of claim 1, wherein the wheeled frame comprises: a wheeled base; a support structure protruding upwards from the wheeled base; and an upper body support attached to the support structure, wherein the upper body support is configured to support at least a portion of the userâs weight and promote an upright posture in the user. 7. The medical walker of claim 6, wherein the support structure is adjustable in length to accommodate users of differing heights. 8. The medical walker of claim 6, wherein the upper body support is a forearm rest. 9. The medical walker of claim 8, wherein the forearm rest comprises: a left arm rest angled to accommodate the userâs left forearm; a right arm rest angled to accommodate the userâs right forearm; a central portion connecting the left arm rest and the right arm rest; and a plurality of handles extending vertically from the center portion. 10. The medical walker of claim 6, wherein the upper body support is an abdominal support. 11. The medical walker of claim 1, further comprising an unweighting system attached to the wheeled frame, the unweighting system configured to support at least a portion of the userâs body weight. 12. The medical walker of claim 11, wherein the unweighting system is adjustable to change an amount of body weight supported by a userâs leg and the compromised limb. 13. The medical walker of claim 12, wherein the unweighting system comprises: a seat; and a seat link that attaches the seat to the wheeled frame at an adjustable height and allows the seat to move laterally. 14. The medical walker of claim 12, wherein the unweighting system comprises: a harness that attaches to the user during use of the medical walker; a series of pulleys and cables, where the pulleys and cables suspend the harness from the wheeled frame; and a fastener that connects the cables to the wheeled frame. 15. The medical walker of claim 14, wherein the fastener is adjustable by a hydraulic system and the hydraulic system comprises: an electric motor; a hydraulic pump powered by the electric motor; a piston cylinder, wherein the hydraulic pump directs hydraulic fluid to control displacement of the piston cylinder; and a bladder accumulator, wherein the hydraulic pump directs the hydraulic fluid into the bladder accumulator and the bladder accumulator supplies a constant pressure to the piston cylinder and the hydraulic pump. 16. A method for using a medical walker by a user with a compromised limb, comprising: positioning the medical walker for connection to a brace, wherein the brace is attached to the compromised limb of the user and the medical walker comprises: a wheeled frame configured to move on a floor surface; and a compromised limb gait system attached to the wheeled frame, where the compromised limb gait system is configured to guide the compromised limb of the user through a simulated gait motion; attaching the brace to the compromised limb gait system; and walking, wherein the compromised limb moves in a simulated gait motion. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the wheeled frame comprises: a wheeled base; a support structure protruding upwards from the wheeled base; and an upper body support attached to the support structure. 18. The method of claim 16, wherein the wheeled frame further comprises an unweighting system attached to the wheeled frame. 19. The method of claim 18, further comprising connecting the user to the unweighting system. 20. A medical walker, comprising: a wheeled frame, wherein the wheeled frame comprises: a wheeled base; a support structure protruding upwards from the wheeled base; an unweighting system attached to the wheeled frame; and an upper body support attached to the stanchion; a compromised limb gait system attached to the wheeled frame, wherein the compromised limb gait system comprises: a yoke, wherein the yoke connects a brace to a compromised limb support system; at least one swing linkage, wherein the swing linkage permits movement in substantially a single plane and connects the yoke to the wheeled frame; and a hard stop, wherein the hard stop limits the rearward motion of the swing linkage.
